subUnits.title: 3.1 Meaning, Identification & Lowest Terms
notes: A fraction represents a part of a whole. It has a numerator (top) and denominator (bottom).
Example: 3/4 means 3 parts out of 4 equal parts of a whole.
To reduce a fraction to its lowest term, divide both numerator and denominator by their Highest Common Factor (HCF).
Fractions with denominator 1 represent whole numbers.

quiz.q: In the fraction 5/9, what is 5 called?
quiz.explanation: The top number is the numerator (parts taken). Bottom is denominator (total parts).
HCF of 8 & 12 is 4. Divide both by 4 → 2/3

quiz.q: Which fraction is already in its lowest term?
quiz.explanation: 5 and 7 share no common factor other than 1.

subUnits.title: 3.2 Equivalent Fractions & Ordering
notes: Equivalent fractions represent the same value but have different numerators and denominators.
Example: 1/2 = 2/4 = 3/6 (multiply top & bottom by same number)
To compare fractions, make denominators equal (find LCM), then compare numerators.
Arrange in ascending order: smallest to largest. Descending: largest to smallest.
Tip: Cross-multiplication can quickly compare two fractions without finding LCM.

quiz.q: If 3/4 = ?/12, what is the missing number?
